originalTitle: States make last-ditch effort to stop the Paramount ‘media behemoth’
summary: A dozen state attorneys general are trying to block the $110 billion merger of Paramount and Warner Bros Discovery they warn would raise movie prices and crush cable TV distributors. The states - California, Arizona, Colorado, Connecticut, Massachusetts, Minnesota, Nevada, New Jersey, New Mexico, New York, Oregon, and Washington - filed suit on Monday, arguing […]
content: A dozen state attorneys general are trying to block the $110 billion merger of Paramount and Warner Bros Discovery they warn would raise movie prices and crush cable TV distributors. The states — California, Arizona, Colorado, Connecticut, Massachusetts, Minnesota, Nevada, New Jersey, New Mexico, New York, Oregon, and Washington — filed suit on Monday, arguing the combination would illegally harm competition and create a “media behemoth.” Last month, the Justice Department declined to block the merger in a decision that The Wall Street Journal reported surprised career staff who were leaning toward recommending a lawsuit. In an exit interview with Politico, former DOJ acting antitrust chief Omeed Assefi rejected the report as “not accurate.” But according to the states’ lawsuit, the merger would result in a consolidation of two out of the five “major film distributors” and basic cable channel owners, meaning that, “for every dollar generated by wide-release theatrical films and basic cable channels in this country, the combined company will pocket more than a quarter.” In a press release, Paramount said the lawsuit was “based on a misrepresentation of competition in the entertainment industry today” and that delaying it would harm consumers and entertainment talent. The merger has raised alarms both in Hollywood and in political circles, in part because it would put CNN under the control of David Ellison, son of close President Donald Trump ally Larry Ellison. Some former CBS journalists have warned of politically charged interference in programming after Ellison tapped The Free Press founder Bari Weiss to head the network, and ratings under Weiss have reportedly dipped.  The lawsuit threatens to be costly for Paramount if it delays the deal from closing by September 30th. It’s committed to a 25 cent per share “ticking fee” to WBD shareholders for each quarter it’s delayed beyond that, about $650 million per quarter.
chineseTitle: 各州尽最后努力阻止派拉蒙“媒体巨头”
chineseSummary: 十几个州总检察长正试图阻止派拉蒙和华纳兄弟探索公司 1100 亿美元的合并，他们警告称这将提高电影价格并压垮有线电视发行商。加利福尼亚州、亚利桑那州、科罗拉多州、康涅狄格州、马萨诸塞州、明尼苏达州、内华达州、新泽西州、新墨西哥州、纽约州、俄勒冈州和华盛顿州等州于周一提起诉讼，辩称[...]
chineseContent: 十几个州总检察长正试图阻止派拉蒙和华纳兄弟探索公司 1100 亿美元的合并，他们警告称这将提高电影价格并压垮有线电视发行商。加利福尼亚州、亚利桑那州、科罗拉多州、康涅狄格州、马萨诸塞州、明尼苏达州、内华达州、新泽西州、新墨西哥州、纽约州、俄勒冈州和华盛顿州周一提起诉讼，称合并将非法损害竞争并创造一个“媒体巨头”。上个月，司法部拒绝阻止合并，《华尔街日报》报道称，这让倾向于建议提起诉讼的职业员工感到惊讶。在接受 Politico 采访时，前司法部代理反垄断主管奥米德·阿塞菲 (Omeed Assefi) 拒绝接受该报告，称其“不准确”。但根据各州的诉讼，合并将导致五家“主要电影发行商”和基本有线电视频道所有者中的两家合并，这意味着“在这个国家广泛发行的院线电影和基本有线电视频道每产生一美元，合并后的公司将获得超过四分之一的收入。”派拉蒙在一份新闻稿中表示，该诉讼“基于对当今娱乐行业竞争的歪曲”，推迟诉讼将损害消费者和娱乐人才的利益。此次合并引起了好莱坞和政界的警惕，部分原因是这将使 CNN 置于唐纳德·特朗普总统亲密盟友拉里·埃里森之子大卫·埃里森的控制之下。埃里森任命《自由报》创始人巴里·韦斯领导该电视台后，一些前哥伦比亚广播公司记者警告说，节目中可能会受到政治干扰，据报道，韦斯领导下的收视率有所下降。  如果派拉蒙推迟 9 月之前完成交易，该诉讼可能会给派拉蒙带来高昂的代价
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于 AI 与科技趋势信号，适合用于观察工具选型、产品机会、开发者关注点和技术路线变化。
